painPoints: Claims processing bottleneck - Manual document handling, slow adjudication, poor communication with claimants
Customer service and inquiry volume - High volume of repetitive questions: coverage details, claims status, policy changes
Policy and claims data fragmentation - Customer data scattered across legacy systems, difficult to access full picture
Compliance and documentation burden - Managing regulatory requirements, audit trails, consent forms, policy documentation
Fraud detection and risk assessment - Limited ability to identify fraud patterns, manual underwriting processes
recommendedProducts: Knowledge Layer - Centralize policy templates, claims procedures, regulatory requirements, compliance documentation
Claims Processing Agent - Automate document ingestion, initial assessment, adjudication support, claimant communication
Customer Service Bot - Answer policy questions, provide claims status, handle policy changes, manage inquiries 24/7
Document Processing Agent - Extract data from claims submissions, policy applications, identify missing information
Risk and Fraud Detection Agent - Analyze claims for fraud patterns, identify high-risk applications, flag exceptions

objections.objection: "Claims decisions are complex—AI can't handle them"
objections.rebuttal: AI doesn't make claims decisions. It gathers information, flags exceptions, presents evidence to underwriters. Humans make the calls with better data faster.
They already communicate with automated systems. They prefer 24/7 status updates over waiting days for a phone call. Complex issues still reach a human.
Compliance is exactly where AI excels. Audit-logged, consistent, documented. Regulatory bodies prefer automated systems with clear decision trails.
False positive rates are less than 5%. Agents focus investigation on real risks. You're catching fraud you currently miss.
We work with legacy systems. Knowledge Layer sits on top, doesn't require replacement. Phased integration over time.
